macro_geo.headline: Fed Signals Steady Rates Amid Inflation Concerns
macro_geo.insight: Federal Reserve minutes indicate no rate cuts in Q2 2026 as core inflation remains sticky at 3.1%, above the 2% target, driven by persistent wage growth and housing costs.

macro_geo.headline: US Proposes New Tariffs on Chinese Tech Imports
macro_geo.insight: US administration plans 25% tariffs on Chinese semiconductors and AI hardware starting May 2026, escalating tensions; China likely to retaliate with rare earth export curbs, risking supply chain disruptions.

macro_geo.headline: Energy Markets Stabilize as OPEC+ Extends Cuts
macro_geo.insight: OPEC+ agrees to maintain production cuts through mid-2026, keeping Brent crude steady at $82/barrel despite rising US shale output; global energy stocks gain 2% on reduced volatility.

regulation.headline: EU AI Act Implementation Progresses with New Compliance Deadlines
regulation.insight: As of March 2026, the EU AI Act, finalized in 2024, enters a critical phase with enforcement deadlines for high-risk AI systems. Companies operating in the EU must now comply with transparency and risk assessment obligations, facing potential fines up to 7% of global revenue. This is driving significant investment in compliance infrastructure, particularly for AI developers in the US and Asia with EU exposure, while sparking debates over innovation stifling.

regulation.headline: US Congress Advances Bipartisan Crypto Regulation Bill Amid SEC Pushback
regulation.insight: In early 2026, US Congress is close to passing a comprehensive crypto regulation framework, aiming to clarify the SEC’s and CFTC’s jurisdictional overlap on digital assets. The bill proposes tailored disclosure rules for crypto issuers and exchanges, but faces criticism from the SEC for perceived leniency on investor protections. If enacted, this could stabilize the US crypto market, though legal challenges are anticipated given ongoing SEC enforcement actions.

hot_takes.take: AI is overhyped and hitting a wall—everyone’s obsessed with generative models, but real-world utility is stalling. Companies are burning cash on LLMs while neglecting practical automation. We’re in a bubble, and by 2027, half these AI startups will be toast.

Markets are sleepwalking into a Fed-induced disaster. Rate cuts are coming too late—recession signals are flashing red with inverted yield curves and slowing job growth. The S&P 500 is due for a 20% correction by Q3 2026. Stop buying the dip.

Crypto is the wildcard no one’s pricing in. Bitcoin’s post-halving rally in 2024 was just the appetizer—adoption by institutions and nation-states will drive it past $200K by 2027. Ignore the noise; this is the ultimate inflation hedge.

Macro obsession with China’s collapse is tired and wrong. Yeah, their property sector’s a mess, but their pivot to tech and green energy is underrated. Western analysts are stuck in 2010—China’s not crashing, it’s reshaping. Fade the doomsday takes.
